subject: Product Management
title: Survivorship Bias in Tests
description: A quick, practical guide to Survivorship Bias in Tests.

hook.text: During WWII, engineers reinforced bomber armor where bullet holes appeared. Abraham Wald said: reinforce where there aren't holes.

buildup.text: Survivorship bias means analyzing only the data that survived — ignoring the missing data that tells the real story.

discovery.text: In product experiments, you only measure users who stuck around. Users who churned before the test ended are invisible.

twist.text: A test might show improved engagement among remaining users while simultaneously increasing churn.

climax.text: The fix: track all users from the moment of assignment, including those who left.

punchline.text: The data you can't see is often more important than the data you can.

quiz.question: How does survivorship bias affect experiment results?
options: Analyzing only users who stayed ignores churned users who may tell the more important story
Survivorship bias makes experiments more accurate
It only affects historical data analysis
